evidence: Algorithm classes (SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator) all decompose work into focused, well-named private phases: fetchCollocatesPerSeed, buildNounToCollocatesMap, filterByMinShared, identifyCoreCollocates — each method is direct and intention-revealing.

Guard clauses appear consistently: BlackLabQueryExecutor guard-returns an empty list for null/empty lemma before any query; VisualizationHandlers validates center/width/height with specific bounds checks; HttpApiUtils.requireParam throws immediately on blank input.

collectFrequenciesAndPosFromGroups(HitGroups, Function<String,String>, Map<String,Long>, Map<String,String>) mutates two caller-provided output maps AND takes a callback — the side-effect footprint is spread across three references, making the call-site less readable than returning a small result record.

VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial is a single 55-line method that parses center, width, height, items array, mode, calls RadialPlot, and sends the response inline — unlike ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers, which both extract a dedicated parse-record private method (parseConcordanceExamplesRequest, parseBcqlRequest) and keep the handler body at 5-8 lines.

CollocateProfileComparator.buildCollocateProfileList correctly comments the unavoidable two-pass variance computation and aggregates sum/min/max in one loop — local state mutation is bounded and the comment explains the trade-off explicitly.

Main.parseNumericParam generic helper avoids duplication across parseInt/parseDouble variants; GrammarConfigLoader decomposes parsing into parseAndValidateVersion, validateNoLegacyKeys, parseRelations, parseRelation — extraction level is balanced and consistent throughout both files.

ComparisonResult.specificTo uses Collectors.toList() (mutable list factory) while the rest of the codebase uniformly uses .toList() (Java-16 immutable list) for terminal stream operations — minor but inconsistent.

strengths: Algorithm classes decompose multi-phase logic into focused, intention-revealing private methods (fetchCollocatesPerSeed, filterByMinShared, etc.) — each phase does one thing and is named after it.
Guard clauses are consistently applied at method entry throughout the query and API layers, making the happy path direct and defensive sprawl minimal.
ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers both extract a typed parse-record private method, keeping their handler bodies slim and the validation logic testable in isolation.
CollocateAccumulator in MultiSeedExplorer is a well-bounded mutable helper that replaces four parallel maps with a single accumulation object, reducing state-mutation surface.
Main.parseNumericParam is a clean generic extraction that eliminates duplication between integer and double parsing while preserving per-caller error messages.
low_level_elegance.issue_character: Issues are localized to two places: one handler that missed the extraction pattern established by its siblings (VisualizationHandlers), and one shared helper whose dual-output-map signature muddles the side-effect boundary (collectFrequenciesAndPosFromGroups) — plus a minor consistency lapse in one model class.
low_level_elegance.score_rationale: The codebase is broadly solid at the function-internals level: phase decomposition is clear, guard clauses are consistent, and the extraction level is balanced across algorithmic and configuration classes. What pulls the score below 80 is a clear asymmetry in the API handler layer — VisualizationHandlers did not follow the extract-parse-record convention that ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers adopted, resulting in a monolith handler body in a small file, and collectFrequenciesAndPosFromGroups mutates two output maps through its signature rather than returning a result type. Both are symptomatic of the same root issue: the refactoring that cleaned up the other handlers was not applied uniformly. A 74 reflects work that is predominantly clean and intention-revealing but with two specific sites where the established local conventions were not carried through.

issues.summary: VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial inlines all request parsing in the handler body instead of extracting a dedicated parse-record method like its siblings
evidence: handleVisualRadial is a single 55-line method that validates center, width, height, iterates and validates the items array, parses mode, calls RadialPlot, and sends the response — all inline.

ConcordanceHandlers extracts parseConcordanceExamplesRequest returning a ConcordanceExamplesRequest record; CorpusQueryHandlers extracts parseBcqlRequest returning a BcqlRequest record; the handler bodies in both are 5-8 lines.
The inline validation branches (if center is null, if width < 1 || width > 5000, etc.) break the control flow of the business action and make the handler harder to test in isolation.
issues.suggestion: Extract a private RadialRequest record and a parseRadialRequest(ObjectNode) method following the sibling pattern. Move all validation (center length, width/height bounds, items loop) into parseRadialRequest; reduce handleVisualRadial to: parse request, call RadialPlot, send response.

issues.summary: collectFrequenciesAndPosFromGroups mutates two caller-provided output maps while also accepting a callback, spreading its side-effect footprint across three parameters
evidence: Signature: collectFrequenciesAndPosFromGroups(HitGroups groups, Function<String,String> lemmaExtractor, Map<String,Long> freqMap, Map<String,String> lemmaPosMap) — callers construct two empty maps, pass them in, then use them after the call.
Three call-sites all follow the same boilerplate: create two maps, call the method, then pass maps to buildAndRankCollocates. The boilerplate could be encapsulated in a small result record.
The callback parameter (lemmaExtractor) combined with the dual-map mutation makes the method contract non-obvious from the signature alone — it both transforms and accumulates.
issues.suggestion: Introduce a private record CollocateData(Map<String,Long> freqMap, Map<String,String> posMap) and change collectFrequenciesAndPosFromGroups to return CollocateData instead of mutating output parameters. Call sites become: var data = collectFrequencies(...); then pass data.freqMap() and data.posMap() to buildAndRankCollocates.

root_causes: The API handler layer was refactored incrementally: ConcordanceHandlers and CorpusQueryHandlers adopted the extract-parse-record convention but VisualizationHandlers was not updated in the same pass.

collectFrequenciesAndPosFromGroups was likely introduced as a quick extraction of shared loop logic; the dual-output-map design preserved the original inline code structure rather than introducing a purpose-built result type.
